# RAPath Philippines

> Philippines FDA / CDRRHR Medical Device Regulatory Reference

Plain English reference for Philippines FDA medical device regulation — CMDN/CMDR pathways, Class A–D classification, abridged ASEAN NRA reliance, and LTO requirements.

RAPath is an independent open-source project providing plain English navigation aids for medical device regulatory requirements. Content is written with AI assistance and derived from publicly available official sources only. Always verify against official regulatory sources before making any regulatory decisions. Not legal or regulatory advice.

## Sections

- [Getting Started](https://rapath.org/ph/getting-started/what-is-a-medical-device)
- [Classification](https://rapath.org/ph/classification/overview)
- [CMDN / CMDR](https://rapath.org/ph/cmdn-cmdr/cmdn-class-a)
- [CDRRHR Ops](https://rapath.org/ph/cdrrhr-operations/establishment-license)
- [Post-Market](https://rapath.org/ph/post-market/overview)
- [Legislation](https://rapath.org/ph/legislation/overview)
- [What's New](https://rapath.org/ph/whats-new/recent-changes)

## Official Sources

- [FDA Philippines — CDRRHR](https://www.fda.gov.ph/center-for-device-regulation-radiation-health-and-research/)
- [FDA Philippines](https://www.fda.gov.ph/)
- [Approved CMDN/CMDR list](https://www.fda.gov.ph/list-of-registered-medical-devices/)
